originalBehavior: The published article loads through a shared article shell plus the page-local base and navarch scripts, then initializes many inline ship, fluid, and propeller scenes under one longform page.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published article structure, shared article styling, route-local ship model assets, and both vendored scripts while rewriting static-hosting metadata and asset paths for the subpath-hosted replica.
notes: The public body no longer exposes the upstream author banner, blog navigation, analytics, or footer supporter prompt.
The route keeps the original longform article structure instead of condensing the content into a custom local rewrite.
evidence: Implementation planning treated the published article shell, the naval model data, and both scripts as parity-critical.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the route loads locally, both scripts initialize, and the buoyancy, stability, free-surface, and propulsion scenes remain interactive.

originalBehavior: The published article opens with pressure and buoyancy demonstrations that link force, depth, submerged volume, and restoring motion through sliders and draggable geometry.
localStatus: The local route keeps the pressure and buoyancy walkthrough intact under the original published runtime.
notes: The smoke contract treats one early pressure or buoyancy path and one drag-based buoyancy scene as required parity behavior.
The route preserves the published progression from pressure to buoyancy instead of flattening those sections into static diagrams.
evidence: The final rollout plan called for one buoyancy drag scene and one early fluid or pressure scene.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the 3D buoyancy-force scene responds to drag and the opening pressure scene updates from slider input.

originalBehavior: The published article continues through hull stability, wind loading, cargo shifts, free-surface effects, waves, and propeller pitch or twist scenes with synchronized sliders, drag state, and animated water overlays.
localStatus: The local route keeps the later stability, free-surface, wave, and propulsion scenes intact through the original article canvas and slider runtime.
notes: The published 3D ship and propeller scenes now load their geometry from the route-local model tree instead of the upstream root path.
The shipped `gears` and `internal-combustion-engine` references are rewritten locally in the public body.
evidence: Implementation planning treated one wind or free-surface scene and one propulsion scene as parity-critical for the naval batch.
Shared smoke coverage verifies the wind-tilt scene, free-surface scene, and propeller-pitch scene update from slider input without runtime asset failures.

originalBehavior: The published article closes with further-reading sections and an upstream footer that includes supporter messaging and author provenance, while also referring back to other Bartosz articles by upstream URL.
localStatus: The local route keeps the published closing sections while leaving the shared footer as the only public original-page reference surface and rewriting available local cross-links.
notes: The local copy uses local fallback font stacks so the route does not depend on runtime Google Fonts requests.
The public body no longer includes the Patreon footer prompt or other author-promo surfaces.
evidence: The hardened Ciechanowski audit flags ordinary in-body upstream article links while allowing footer-only provenance.
Route cleanup rewrote the local `gears` and `internal-combustion-engine` references so the family continues converging toward local intra-family navigation.
